note: March at Aihole: 20–34°C, last cool window for the Chalukya temple cluster. Morning walks between the 120+ shrines work; by noon the stone radiates heat — aim for dawn arrivals.
March on the Alleppey backwaters: 25–33°C, houseboats still pleasant at dawn and dusk but midday sun bites. Shoulder to peak — canal breezes help, AC houseboats worth it.
March at Auroville: 24–33°C, last good month before heat sets in. Matrimandir bookings easier than peak winter, community kitchens and forest cycle paths still workable before noon.
March at Bekal: 25–33°C, fort walks comfortable early morning, Arabian Sea breeze holds through the day. Crowds thinner than Dec–Feb peak — easier to photograph the bastions empty.
March at Beypore: 25–33°C, Uru shipyard visits workable before noon. Port-side heat builds by afternoon — pair a morning yard walk with a late beach return.
March at Bidar: 18–34°C, last cool stretch for the fort and Bidriware workshops. Deccan plateau mornings still crisp; afternoons warm — plan the Madrasa and tombs early.
March in Chennai: 24–33°C, Marina walks and Fort St George still workable before mid-morning. Coastal humidity starts rising — peak is Dec–Feb, but temple and museum circuits hold up.
March at Cherai: 25–33°C, long sands and dolphin sightings continue though swims feel warm by afternoon. Thinner than Feb peak — family stays easier to book.
March in Chettinad: 24–36°C, mansion tours fine early, but inland Tamil sun is sharp by noon. Food and tile photography hold up — book AC rooms, eat long lunches.
March at Chidambaram: 24–34°C, Nataraja temple darshan comfortable at dawn and after 5pm. Midday stone courtyards get hot — dress light, carry water.
March at Chitradurga: 20–35°C, last workable month for the seven-walled fort climb. Boulder terrain heats fast — start at sunrise, finish before 10am.
March at Coonoor: 12–24°C, Nilgiri tea slopes green and clear, toy train running. Shoulder to peak — quieter than April–May, homestays easy to book.
March at Daman: 22–33°C, Portuguese fort walks and beaches still pleasant. Gujarat coast warming but tolerable — peak crowds have eased after the winter rush.
March at Deomali: 18–32°C, last comfortable trekking window before humidity builds. Dongria Kondh country dry and open — start summits pre-dawn to beat the sun.
March at Dhanushkodi: 26–34°C, the ghost-town ruins and sandspit still driveable. Sun is unrelenting with zero shade — go before 9am or after 4pm.
March at Diu: 22–32°C, Portuguese fort and island beaches comfortable. Shoulder season — fewer Gujarati weekenders than Feb, rooms easier to find last-minute.
March at Gulbarga: 19–35°C, Bahmani fort and Sufi dargahs workable before noon. Deccan sun hits hard by midday — plan the roofed Jama Masjid for late afternoon.
March at Guruvayur: 25–34°C, temple darshan crowded but orderly. Kerala coast humidity rising — early-morning pradakshina is the comfortable window.
March at Idukki: 18–30°C, cardamom hills clear, Periyar dam viewpoints dry and workable. Shoulder to peak — spice estates quieter than Dec, wildlife sightings steady.
March at Kanchipuram: 24–35°C, temple tours and silk-weaver visits best before 10am. Plains heat stacks fast — the 1,000-temple circuit needs an AC car between stops.
March at Kannur: 25–33°C, last stretch of the Theyyam season in some shrines. Malabar beaches still enjoyable — sea breeze tempers the rising coastal humidity.
March at Kanyakumari: 25–33°C, sunrise and sunset over three seas still work. Mid-day heat at the Vivekananda Rock is fierce — ferry queues ease vs Dec peak.
March at Karwar: 24–33°C, Konkan beaches and Devbagh Island boats still pleasant. Shoulder season — calmer than Christmas week, dolphin rides easy to arrange.
March at Fort Kochi: 25–33°C, Chinese Fishing Nets and heritage lanes workable early and evening. Biennale has wound down; humidity rising — cafe-hopping over long walks.
March at Kodaikanal: 10–22°C, Coaker's Walk clear, lake boating pleasant. Shoulder to peak — quieter than April–May summer rush, homestays easier to book.
March at Kollam: 25–33°C, Ashtamudi Lake and the 8-hour Alleppey cruise still work. Canoe rides on Munroe Island best at dawn before humidity builds.
March in Konaseema: 24–35°C, Godavari delta mornings still cool on coconut-fringed canals. Heat builds fast by afternoon — shift to village stays with shaded verandahs.
March at Kotagiri: 12–24°C, oldest Nilgiri slopes clear and tea-bloomed. Quieter than Ooty across the season — easy homestay bookings before April's school-holiday rush.
March at Kovalam: 25–33°C, lighthouse beach still good for short swims and ayurveda stays. Peak international season winding down — better room rates, thinner sunbed queues.
March at Kozhikode: 25–33°C, Malabar food trail and Kappad beach workable. Coastal humidity climbing — biryani lunches over long walking tours.
March at Kumarakom: 25–33°C, Vembanad Lake houseboats still pleasant though Siberian birds have largely left. Shoulder to peak — premium stays cheaper than Dec–Jan.
March at Kumbakonam: 24–35°C, the 188 temples and Airavatesvara (UNESCO) best before 10am. Cauvery delta heat builds fast — plan filter-coffee breaks between darshans.
March at Kumbalangi: 25–33°C, canal cycling and crab-farm visits workable early. Eco-village stays quieter than peak Dec — families get better attention from hosts.
March in Kuttanad: 25–33°C, below-sea-level paddy fields green before harvest. Shikara canal rides work dawn and late afternoon — midday sun on the water is harsh.
March at Laknavaram: 20–35°C, suspension-bridge mornings still cool over the 13 forested islands. Afternoon heat builds fast — pair sunrise boats with a forest lunch.
March at Machilipatnam: 24–34°C, Kalamkari workshop visits still comfortable in the morning. Coastal AP heat climbs by noon — plan studio time, then a late port stroll.
March at Madurai: 24–36°C, Meenakshi temple darshan best at dawn or after 5pm. Midnight closing ritual still runs — temple interiors stay cool, streets don't.
March at Mahabalipuram: 25–34°C, shore temples and Pallava rock-cuts workable early. Coromandel sun is direct — budget shaded breaks at the seafood shacks.
March at Malvan: 24–33°C, scuba visibility on the Sindhudurg reef still decent. Shoulder to peak — dive slots and Malvani kitchens easier to book than peak Dec.
March at Mandrem: 24–33°C, yoga retreats winding down, kitesurfing wind weakening. Quieter than Feb peak — beach shacks thin out, room rates drop meaningfully.
March at Mandvi: 20–33°C, last comfortable month for the palace and dhow-building yards. Kutch coast warming — morning yard visits, afternoon palace shade.
March at Marari Beach: 25–33°C, fishing-village calm holds, seas still swimmable early. Shoulder season — eco-stays quieter than Dec peak, sunbed crowds minimal.
March at Marine NP: 22–33°C, low-tide reef walks still workable with forest dept permits. Jamnagar coast warming fast — pre-dawn starts are non-negotiable.
March at Minicoy: 26–31°C, lagoon snorkels and lighthouse climbs pleasant. Tail end of the good window before pre-monsoon swells arrive — ferry schedules can shift.
March at Morjim: 24–33°C, olive-ridley nesting ending, Russian-season crowds thinning. Quiet-luxury mode returns — beach yoga and seafood shacks still working.
March at Munnar: 12–26°C, tea estates green, Eravikulam tahrs active. Shoulder to peak — quieter than April–May school break, homestay rates reasonable.
March at Murudeshwar: 25–33°C, Shiva statue and headland viewpoints workable all day with sea breeze. Snorkel boats to Netrani still running — peak is Nov–Feb.
March at Nalsarovar: 20–34°C, migratory birds largely departed but resident flamingos and pelicans hold. Shoulder month — fewer boats on the wetland, dawn light still strong.
March at Nelliyampathy: 15–28°C, plantation walks and birding pleasant in the hills. Dry-season clarity strong — a quieter alternative to Munnar's peak bookings.
March at Ooty: 10–22°C, botanical gardens and toy train clear, pre-summer calm. Shoulder to peak — April–May brings the summer rush, book before schools break.
March in Pondicherry: 25–34°C, French Quarter cafes and Rock Beach workable early and evening. Midday humidity rising — shift to promenade walks after 5pm.
March at Ponmudi: 16–28°C, butterfly season active, 22 hairpins clear. Shoulder — weekends from Trivandrum still busy, weekdays nearly empty.
March at Poovar: 25–33°C, estuary and island-resort boats still pleasant. Shoulder to peak — room rates below Dec–Jan, river cruises quieter at dawn.
March at Raghurajpur: 22–35°C, Pattachitra artist homes welcoming in cool mornings. Village walks comfortable before 10am — heat builds sharply by noon.
March at Rameswaram: 26–33°C, Pamban bridge crossings and temple corridor still workable. Peak pilgrimage calms after Magh — darshan queues thinner than Feb.
March at Reis Magos: 24–33°C, restored Portuguese fort walks with Mandovi breeze. Shoulder to Goa peak — gallery quieter than Feb, ferry crossings easy.
March at Sasan Gir: 20–36°C, forest dept safaris still running with solid lion sightings at waterholes. Peak dry-season tracking — afternoon slots are hot, go at dawn.
March at Srirangam: 25–35°C, temple corridors cooler than the streets outside. Plan the 7-wall circuit at dawn or after 5pm — granite holds heat through afternoon.
March at Thanjavur: 24–35°C, Brihadeeswara (UNESCO) best in the early hour before the vimana shadow vanishes at noon. Cauvery delta warming — pace heritage stops carefully.
March at Tharangambadi: 25–33°C, Fort Dansborg and the Danish quarter workable with sea breeze. Shoulder month — beach calm, bungalow stays easier to book.
March at Thekkady: 16–30°C, Periyar lake boat safaris still productive at elephants' drinking hours. Spice plantations aromatic in dry air — shoulder to peak, book ahead for weekends.
March at Thrissur: 25–35°C, temple towns and Kerala Mural Institute walks best before noon. Pooram buildup visible in the streets — big festival usually lands April/May.
March at Trichy: 24–35°C, Rock Fort climb comfortable at dawn, scorching by midday. Srirangam close by — stack both sites early, retreat to AC after lunch.
March in Trivandrum: 25–34°C, Padmanabhaswamy darshan and palace tours workable morning-only. Kerala capital humidity rising — pace city stops with long coffee breaks.
March at Udupi: 25–33°C, Krishna Temple pooja and Malpe Beach both pleasant. Shoulder to Karnataka coast peak — hotel Woodlands–style lunches land well in the heat.
March at Vagamon: 15–28°C, pine forests and paragliding meadows clear. Shoulder — weekend Kochi crowds real, weekdays you get the meadows mostly to yourself.
March at Varkala: 25–33°C, cliff beach and ayurveda stays still working. International season winding down — better rates, yoga classes less packed than Jan–Feb.
March at Velavadar: 20–35°C, last comfortable month for blackbuck sightings in open grassland. Forest dept safaris running — dawn slots before the sun flattens the grass.
March at Wayanad: 16–32°C, heart-shaped lake and Edakkal caves workable. Shoulder to peak — spice estates quieter than Dec, weekend jams from Kochi still real.
March at Yercaud: 14–26°C, coffee-estate walks clear, lake breezes cool. Shoulder — quieter than April–May summer rush, budget homestays easy to book.
October at Aihole: 20–32°C, post-monsoon Malaprabha valley green and dust settled. Chalukya temples photograph well in softer light — shoulder to peak heritage season.
October in Alleppey: 24–31°C, monsoon hangover clearing, backwaters fuller than peak winter. Solid conditions — peak houseboat season builds through Nov–Feb.
October at Athirapally: 22–30°C, falls still roaring with post-monsoon volume, forest green. Works well — drier than Aug-Sep, peak crowds arrive Nov-Dec.
October at Beypore: 24–31°C, post-monsoon seas calmer, Uru shipyard back to full schedule. Malabar coast still humid but workable — peak is Dec–Feb.
October at Bhavnagar: 22–35°C, Saurashtra warming post-monsoon. Temple and museum circuits workable — Gulf of Cambay breeze tempers the heat. Peak lands Nov–Feb.
October at Champaner-Pavagadh: 22–34°C, UNESCO mosques and Pavagadh climb workable with post-monsoon greenery. Shoulder — peak archaeological-tour season starts Nov.
October at Courtallam: 22–32°C, falls still flowing from southwest monsoon tail, northeast monsoon building. Shoulder — bathing therapy season peaks Jun–Sep, Oct quieter.
October at Daman: 24–33°C, post-monsoon coast clearer, Portuguese forts workable. Shoulder to peak — beach crowds arrive Nov after Diwali.
October at Diu: 24–32°C, island cooling post-monsoon, fort and church circuits pleasant. Shoulder — Gujarati weekender rush builds through Nov.
October at Dwarka: 24–34°C, Char Dham pilgrimage rhythm steady, Arabian Sea breeze holds. Shoulder to peak — full winter crowds build Nov–Feb.
October at Eravikulam: 10–22°C, park reopens after monsoon closure, Nilgiri tahrs visible on grassy slopes. Shoulder — peak Neelakurinji years aside, Nov–Feb draws bigger crowds.
October in Gandhinagar: 22–34°C, Akshardham and Adalaj stepwell tours workable as Gujarat cools. Tree-lined boulevards start showing post-monsoon green — peak lands Nov–Jan.
October at Gulbarga: 21–33°C, Bahmani fort and Sufi dargahs workable as Deccan plateau cools. Shoulder — dry tombs and fully-roofed Jama Masjid comfortable before peak winter.
October at Guruvayur: 24–31°C, Kerala post-monsoon humidity easing. Temple elephants visible in cooler early hours — shoulder to peak darshan season.
October at Junagadh: 22–34°C, Girnar climb and Buddhist caves workable post-monsoon. Ashokan edict walks pleasant — peak pilgrim and trekking season kicks off Nov.
October at Kannur: 24–31°C, Malabar monsoon winding down, Theyyam season yet to start. Shoulder — beaches workable, peak ritual and fort tourism lands Dec–Feb.
October at Fort Kochi: 24–31°C, post-monsoon humidity lifting, Chinese Fishing Nets working their rhythm. Shoulder — biennale years aside, peak is Dec–Feb.
October at Kollam: 24–31°C, Ashtamudi Lake full post-monsoon, the 8-hour Alleppey cruise back in regular rotation. Shoulder to peak — canoe tours pleasant.
October at Kozhikode: 24–32°C, post-monsoon Malabar cooling, Kappad beach calmer. Shoulder — biryani trail workable, peak food-tour season builds Nov–Feb.
October at Kumarakom: 24–31°C, Vembanad Lake brimming, Siberian birds starting to arrive. Solid pre-peak window — premium stays cheaper than Dec–Jan rates.
October at Kumbalangi: 24–31°C, monsoon has greened the canals, eco-village back to full rhythm. Shoulder — crab farms productive, homestays start filling through Nov.
October in Kuttanad: 24–31°C, paddy fields green post-monsoon, canal levels full. Shikara rides work in early morning and late afternoon — peak houseboat season builds through Nov.
October at Lothal: 22–34°C, Indus Valley dockyard workable as Gujarat cools. Morning site walks comfortable — archaeological museum is AC-cool any hour.
October at Mandrem: 24–32°C, Goa shoulder month — monsoon behind, high season yet to arrive. Yoga retreats reopening, beach shacks rebuilding — quieter than Nov–Jan rush.
October at Mandvi: 22–33°C, Kutch coast cooling post-monsoon. Palace and dhow yards workable — shoulder to peak Rann-of-Kutch season which starts mid-Nov.
October at Meghamalai: 14–24°C, cloud forest still damp from monsoon tail, tea estates clear between mist. Advance booking essential — peak clarity builds through Nov–Dec.
October at Modhera: 22–34°C, Sun Temple carvings workable post-monsoon. Equinox light still close enough to fall into the sanctum — pair with Patan stepwell same day.
October at Nalsarovar: 20–33°C, migratory birds starting to arrive, wetland levels high post-monsoon. Shoulder — flamingo numbers build meaningfully through Nov–Jan.
October at Poovar: 24–31°C, estuary full post-monsoon, island-resort boats back to regular schedule. Shoulder to peak — room rates below Dec premium.
October at Porbandar: 23–34°C, Gandhi's birthplace walks workable as Saurashtra cools. Arabian Sea breeze tempers the heat — shoulder to peak coastal season.
October at Rani ki Vav: 22–34°C, UNESCO stepwell carvings glow in post-monsoon softer light. Morning descents comfortable — 500+ sculptures best studied before noon.
October at Saputara: 16–28°C, Western Ghats still green from monsoon, waterfalls flowing. Shoulder — Gujarat's only hill station in pleasant post-rain clarity, peak is Nov–Jan.
October at Statue of Unity: 22–34°C, Narmada valley green post-monsoon, viewing gallery clear. Shoulder — busier than low season, quieter than Nov–Feb peak.
October in Surat: 24–34°C, post-monsoon humidity easing, food streets back to full rhythm. Shoulder — diamond-market tours workable, peak tourism builds Nov–Jan.
October in Trivandrum: 24–31°C, monsoon tail clearing, palaces and zoo workable. Shoulder — Padmanabhaswamy queues thinner than Dec, pace city circuits in morning.
October at Valparai: 15–26°C, cloud forests still misty from monsoon, 40 hairpins clear of slides. Shoulder — lion-tailed macaque sightings active, peak dry-season is Dec–Feb.
November at Adilabad: 15–30°C, Kuntala Falls thinned but tiger reserve dry and productive. Gond tribal villages workable — wildlife over waterfalls this month.
November in Alleppey: 23–31°C, backwater houseboat season fully underway, canals full. Solid pre-Christmas window — rates below Dec–Jan peak, weather stable.
November at Ambaji: 16–30°C, Shakti Peeth climb workable as Gujarat cools. Shoulder to peak pilgrimage season — Rajasthan border breezes temper the plateau heat.
November at Athirapally: 22–30°C, falls still flowing strongly from northeast monsoon, forest deep green. Shoulder — peak dry-season visits come Dec–Feb when viewpoints drain.
November at Auroville: 23–31°C, Matrimandir bookings easier than peak Dec–Jan, community kitchens in full rhythm. Solid month — northeast monsoon showers possible.
November at Bekal: 23–31°C, fort and Arabian Sea coast workable as Kerala post-monsoon humidity eases. Solid pre-peak window — homestays still reasonably priced.
November in Chennai: 23–30°C, northeast monsoon active — Marina works between showers. Temple and music-season buildup begins — peak Margazhi concerts land Dec–Jan.
November at Cherai: 23–31°C, dolphin boats and Chinese fishing nets workable, Kerala humidity easing. Shoulder to peak family-beach season — quieter than Dec rush.
November in Chettinad: 22–31°C, mansion tours and tile photography in post-monsoon softer light. Shoulder — food-tour season building, book hosted meals ahead.
November at Chidambaram: 23–30°C, Nataraja temple courtyards pleasant with northeast monsoon showers. Peak Arudra Darshan lands Dec — Nov is calmer darshan window.
November at Chinnar: 18–30°C, driest sanctuary in Kerala now accessible, Grizzled Giant Squirrel active. Shoulder — peak trekking season builds Dec–Feb.
November in Coimbatore: 20–30°C, Adiyogi statue and Isha visits workable with northeast monsoon showers. Gateway to Nilgiris — peak Ooty rush still a month away.
November at Courtallam: 22–30°C, falls still flowing on northeast monsoon, herb-infused bathing still productive. Shoulder — peak bathing season was Jun–Sep.
November at Dakor: 16–30°C, Ranchhodrai temple pilgrimage rhythm steady as Gujarat cools. Krishna-devotee crowds dense on Ekadashi — regular days easy.
November at Dhanushkodi: 24–31°C, ghost-town drives workable with northeast monsoon cooling. Shoulder — peak desert-sand photography weather builds Dec–Jan.
November at Dholavira: 16–32°C, Harappan site visits workable as Kutch cools. Shoulder to peak — Rann festival crowds start building mid-Nov.
November at Eravikulam: 10–22°C, Nilgiri tahrs clear on the slopes, Anamudi views holding. Shoulder — peak tahr-season tourism builds Dec–Feb with school holidays.
November at Gir NP: 16–32°C, forest dept safaris running with solid Asiatic lion sightings. Shoulder to peak — dry-season tracking improves through Dec–Apr.
November at Kadmat: 24–30°C, lagoon diving and kayaking workable, sandbank stable. Shoulder to peak Lakshadweep window — permits still needed, ferry schedules holding.
November at Kanchipuram: 22–31°C, temple tours and silk-weaver visits workable with northeast monsoon showers. Shoulder — peak festival season builds Dec–Jan.
November at Kanyakumari: 24–30°C, sunrise and sunset over three seas with post-monsoon clarity. Vivekananda Rock ferry queues thinner than Dec peak.
November at Khandala: 15–28°C, Western Ghats greens still holding from monsoon, Duke's Nose clear. Shoulder — weekend Mumbai crowds real, weekdays calm.
November at Kollam: 23–31°C, Ashtamudi Lake full, Munroe Island canoe rides pleasant, Alleppey cruise running. Solid backwater window before Dec premium.
November at Kovalam: 23–31°C, lighthouse beach swimmable, ayurveda stays reopening for season. Shoulder to peak — better rates than Dec–Jan international rush.
November at Kumarakom: 23–31°C, Vembanad Lake brimming, first Siberian birds arriving. Shoulder to peak — houseboats priced below Dec premium, luxury stays filling.
November at Kumbakonam: 22–31°C, 188 temples and Airavatesvara (UNESCO) workable with northeast monsoon cooling. Shoulder — Karthigai Deepam crowds possible mid-month.
November at Kumbalangi: 23–31°C, eco-village canals full, crab farms productive. Shoulder to peak — homestays filling for Dec, book a month ahead for weekends.
November in Kuttanad: 23–31°C, paddy fields turning toward harvest, canals full. Shikara rides calm — shoulder to peak houseboat season which lands Dec–Feb.
November at Madurai: 22–31°C, Meenakshi temple workable through the day with northeast monsoon showers. Shoulder — peak temple season lands Dec–Jan with Margazhi crowds.
November at Mahabalipuram: 23–30°C, Pallava rock-cuts and shore temple pleasant with post-monsoon clarity. Shoulder to peak — beach-heritage combo holds up.
November at Marari: 23–31°C, fishing village calm, seas swimmable, boutique eco-stays filling. Solid pre-peak window — rates below Dec–Jan international season.
November at Marine NP: 20–32°C, low-tide coral walks workable with forest dept permit. Shoulder to peak marine-viewing window which holds through Feb.
November at Meghamalai: 13–22°C, cloud forest clearer as northeast monsoon eases, tea estates visible between drifts. Advance booking essential — peak clarity is Dec–Jan.
November at Minicoy: 25–30°C, lagoon snorkels and lighthouse climbs back to peak conditions. Shoulder to peak Lakshadweep window — ferry schedules steady, permits required.
November at Point Calimere: 22–30°C, flamingos starting to flock, blackbuck visible on the Ramsar wetland. Shoulder — peak flocks build through Dec–Feb.
November in Pondicherry: 23–30°C, French Quarter cafes workable between northeast monsoon showers. Shoulder to peak — Rock Beach promenade calmer than Dec rush.
November at Rameswaram: 24–30°C, Pamban bridge crossings and temple corridor workable with sea breeze. Shoulder — peak Char Dham pilgrim crowds build Dec–Feb.
November at Saputara: 14–26°C, Western Ghats dry out, tribal villages welcoming. Solid pre-peak window — Gujarat winter holiday crowds still weeks away.
November at Sasan Gir: 16–32°C, forest dept safaris productive with animals at waterholes. Shoulder to peak — Maldhari homestays and eco-resorts filling for Dec.
November at Silent Valley: 16–28°C, rainforest still drying post-monsoon, forest dept trek permits issued. Shoulder — peak dry-season birding builds Dec–Feb.
November at Srirangam: 22–31°C, Ranganathaswamy temple corridors workable with northeast monsoon cooling. Shoulder — peak Vaikunta Ekadashi crowds land Dec–Jan.
November at Thanjavur: 22–31°C, Brihadeeswara (UNESCO) workable with post-monsoon softer light. Cauvery delta cooling — peak Chola-heritage season builds Dec–Feb.
November at Tharangambadi: 23–30°C, Fort Dansborg and Danish quarter workable with post-monsoon sea breeze. Shoulder — heritage bungalow stays easier to book before Dec.
November at Thrissur: 22–31°C, temple circuits and Kerala Mural Institute workable with northeast monsoon cooling. Shoulder — peak Pooram buildup is April/May.

November at Valparai: 14–24°C, cloud forests drying post-monsoon, 40 hairpins clear. Shoulder to peak — lion-tailed macaque sightings productive, Dec–Feb holds peak clarity.
November at Varkala: 23–31°C, cliff beach and ayurveda stays reopening for season. Shoulder to peak — better rates than Dec–Jan international rush.
November at Vedanthangal: 22–30°C, waterbirds starting to nest, northeast monsoon filling the tanks. Shoulder — peak flocks build Dec–Feb, 40,000 birds by mid-winter.
